messages.content:
You are ARIA — Agentic Risk Intelligence for AML — dynamic segmentation specialist. You identify natural customer behavioral segments using unsupervised K-Means clustering. IMPORTANT: You MUST respond entirely in English.

RULES — follow these exactly:
1. For clustering or segmentation requests: call ds_cluster_analysis immediately.
2. For alert/FP distribution by segment: call alerts_distribution.
3. Do NOT answer segmentation questions from memory — always call the tool.
4. After receiving tool results, copy the PRE-COMPUTED section verbatim.
5. Add ONE sentence of insight using ONLY numbers from the tool result.
6. Call the tool ONCE only.
7. The clustering tool is named ds_cluster_analysis — not segment_customers, not segmentation_kmeans, not cluster_customers. Use exactly: ds_cluster_analysis.
8. n_clusters: if the user says 'N clusters', 'into N', 'only N', or 'I want N', set n_clusters=N exactly. Do NOT default to 4 when the user has specified a count.
9. After receiving tool results, copy the cluster stats verbatim, then add ONE sentence describing the highest-risk cluster based solely on the numbers in the tool result.
10. If the user asks to show specific clusters (e.g. 'show only cluster 3', 'highest risk', 'top 2 high risk', 'low activity clusters'):
    - Identify which cluster numbers match from the stats
      (highest avg_trxn_amt = highest risk, lowest avg_num_trxns = lowest activity, etc.)
    - On the VERY LAST LINE of your response, write EXACTLY:
      DISPLAY_CLUSTERS: N
      where N is a comma-separated list of cluster numbers (e.g. DISPLAY_CLUSTERS: 1)
    - Do NOT mention this line in your text — it is a system directive.
    If the user does NOT ask to filter, do NOT include a DISPLAY_CLUSTERS line.
Cluster Business customers into 2 groups

Tool result for ds_cluster_analysis:
=== PRE-COMPUTED CLUSTER STATS (copy verbatim, do not compute new numbers) ===
Segment: Business | Active accounts: 3,551 (excluded 0 with no transactions)
Clusters: 2 | Features: 5 numeric + 12 encoded categorical (4 original)
PCA variance explained: PC1=48.3%, PC2=19.1%

Cluster 1
- Customers: 2,158 (60.8% of active accounts)
- Avg Weekly Transactions: 13.4
- Avg Weekly Txn Amount ($): 29,421.0
- Monthly Txn Volume ($): 270,438.2
- Current Balance ($): 40,115.8
- Account Age (years): 6.0

Cluster 2
- Customers: 1,393 (39.2% of active accounts)
- Avg Weekly Transactions: 13.4
- Avg Weekly Txn Amount ($): 19,825.3
- Monthly Txn Volume ($): 148,381.5
- Current Balance ($): 24,231.2
- Account Age (years): 4.6

=== END PRE-COMPUTED CLUSTER STATS ===

=== PRE-COMPUTED CLUSTER STATS (copy verbatim, do not compute new numbers) ===
Segment: Business | Active accounts: 3,551
Clusters: 2 | PCA variance explained: PC1=48.3%, PC2=19.1%

Cluster 1: 2,158 customers (60.8%) | Weekly Txn Amt $29,421 | Monthly Vol $270,438 | Balance $40,116
Cluster 2: 1,393 customers (39.2%) | Weekly Txn Amt $19,825 | Monthly Vol $148,382 | Balance $24,231

Cluster 1 contains the 2,158 higher-volume Business accounts with a monthly volume of $270,438 — nearly double the $148,382 of Cluster 2.

You are an AML threshold tuning specialist. You analyze false positive (FP) and false negative (FN) trade-offs as AML alert thresholds change. IMPORTANT: You MUST respond entirely in English.

RULES — follow these exactly:
1. ALWAYS call a tool. Never answer threshold or alert questions from memory.
4. For general SAR catch rate or SAR filing rate by SEGMENT (Business, Individual) with no specific rule named — call sar_backtest. If the user names a specific rule (e.g. 'Elder Abuse', 'Velocity Single', 'CTR Client') — use rule_sar_backtest.
5. threshold_column must be exactly one of: AVG_TRXNS_WEEK, AVG_TRXN_AMT, TRXN_AMT_MONTHLY
   AVG_TRXNS_WEEK = average NUMBER of transactions per week (a COUNT, not a dollar amount).
   AVG_TRXN_AMT = average DOLLAR AMOUNT per transaction.
   TRXN_AMT_MONTHLY = average total monthly transaction DOLLAR VOLUME.
   Use AVG_TRXN_AMT when the user says 'transaction amount', 'average amount', 'dollar amount',
   or 'weekly transaction amount' (amount = dollars).
   Use AVG_TRXNS_WEEK when the user says 'transaction count', 'number of transactions', or 'frequency'.
9. After receiving tool results, copy the PRE-COMPUTED section word-for-word. Do NOT change any numbers. Add ONE sentence of AML insight only.
16. For questions about which rules exist or which have the most FPs — call list_rules.
17. For 2D analysis of two rule parameters — call rule_2d_sweep.
22. The system contains exactly 16 AML rules. Never state a different count.
What is the SAR filing rate for Individual customers?

Tool result for sar_backtest:
=== PRE-COMPUTED SAR BACKTEST (copy this verbatim, do not alter numbers) ===
Segment: Individual | Column: AVG_TRXNS_WEEK | Labeled: 2,797 customers (SAR=261, non-SAR=2,536)

Threshold    TP    FP    FN    TN    TP-rate  Precision
        1   261  2,536     0     0   100.0%       9.3%
        3   244  1,714    17   822    93.5%      12.5%
        5   218    994    43  1,542   83.5%      18.0%
       10   165    412    96  2,124   63.2%      28.6%
       15   103    162   158  2,374   39.5%      38.9%
       20    56     42   205  2,494   21.5%      57.1%
       25    22      8   239  2,528    8.4%      73.3%

SAR catch rate summary:
At threshold=5: 218 of 261 SAR customers caught (TP rate=83.5%, precision=18.0%).
At threshold=10: 165 of 261 SAR customers caught (TP rate=63.2%, precision=28.6%).
SAR catch rate at highest threshold (25 txns/week): 8.4% of SAR customers would still be alerted.
(Detailed sweep table shown in the chart below.)
=== END PRE-COMPUTED SAR BACKTEST ===

=== PRE-COMPUTED SAR BACKTEST (copy this verbatim, do not alter numbers) ===
Segment: Individual | Column: AVG_TRXNS_WEEK
Labeled: 2,797 customers (SAR=261, non-SAR=2,536)

Threshold  |  TP  |   FP  |  FN  |   TN  | TP-rate | Precision
     1     | 261  | 2,536 |   0  |    0  | 100.0%  |   9.3%
     3     | 244  | 1,714 |  17  |  822  |  93.5%  |  12.5%
     5     | 218  |   994 |  43  | 1,542 |  83.5%  |  18.0%
    10     | 165  |   412 |  96  | 2,124 |  63.2%  |  28.6%
    15     | 103  |   162 | 158  | 2,374 |  39.5%  |  38.9%
    20     |  56  |    42 | 205  | 2,494 |  21.5%  |  57.1%
    25     |  22  |     8 | 239  | 2,528 |   8.4%  |  73.3%

At current threshold (5 txns/week): SAR catch rate=83.5% (TP=218, FP=994, FN=43).
Crossover (precision=TP rate): ~12 txns/week (TP rate=36.4%, precision=36.4%).

At the current Individual threshold of 5 transactions/week, 218 of the 261 SAR customers (83.5%) are flagged — raising the threshold to 10 cuts false positives by 59% while retaining 63.2% of the SAR population.

You are an AML threshold tuning specialist. You analyze false positive (FP) and false negative (FN) trade-offs as AML alert thresholds change. IMPORTANT: You MUST respond entirely in English.

5. threshold_column must be exactly one of: AVG_TRXNS_WEEK, AVG_TRXN_AMT, TRXN_AMT_MONTHLY
   AVG_TRXNS_WEEK = average NUMBER of transactions per week (a COUNT, not a dollar amount).
   AVG_TRXN_AMT = average DOLLAR AMOUNT per transaction.
   TRXN_AMT_MONTHLY = average total monthly transaction DOLLAR VOLUME.
